values: UBC Mathematics Undergraduate Research Projects — 2026 Projects
Public institutional emails were verified against current UBC or UBC-hosted directory/profile pages. Project synopses are concise paraphrases of the official listing.
Mathematical models to understand the effects of disease-induced mortality after recovery
Develop differential-equation models to study how elevated mortality after recovery affects epidemic oscillations, long-term population dynamics, and the evolution of post-infection mortality.
Mathematical modelling of interactions between infectious-disease dynamics and human behaviour
Couple epidemiological and evolutionary game-theory models to investigate the two-way feedback between disease transmission and individual decision-making.
Extend prior models to examine how uncertain immune strength, duration, accumulation, severity protection, vaccine schedules, and unequal vaccine access shape medium- and long-term COVID-19 dynamics.
AI/ML; computational optimal transport; climate and data systems
Work with UBC or industry collaborators on projects such as image and shape analysis, optimal transport, climate modelling, or database and dashboard development.
Particle assemblies; rigidity; geometry and simulation
Study configuration spaces of ellipse or ellipsoid packings—their rigidity, topology, and geometry—and potentially run molecular-dynamics simulations of interacting particles.
Inverse and Forward Design of Shape-Morphing Programmable Materials
Soft materials; geometric mechanics; numerical design

Develop numerical schemes that either predict a programmable material's shape from its design or solve the inverse problem of finding a configuration that produces a desired shape or mechanical response.
Geometric Phase Space and Information Density of Multi-Mode Materials
Explore design strategies for materials programmable into multiple shapes by studying trajectories and information density in their geometric phase space.
Model autonomous shape-morphing materials driven by chemical reactions, focusing on how an evolving curved surface changes the spatiotemporal behaviour of the reaction.
Distributionally Robust Optimization for Materials Design
Build a stochastic and distributionally robust optimization framework for designing disordered lattices whose macroscopic properties remain high-performing, isotropic, and resilient to defects.
The 3-Pfister Number of a Quadratic Form
Use a combinatorial model of Witt rings over a tractable class of fields to study bounds on the number of 3-fold Pfister forms needed to represent quadratic forms.
Modelling cell migration in confinement: coupling differential geometry, fluid mechanics and reaction-diffusion systems
Mathematical biology; evolving-surface PDEs; finite elements
Formulate and solve coupled surface-PDE, mechanics, and fluid models for cells migrating through deformable confined environments, then compare simulations with collaborators' experiments.
Undergraduate Research Opportunity in Fluid Mechanics and Rheology
Ian Frigaard; Yosef Rezaei; Soheil Akbari
Combine complex-fluid preparation, rheology, imaging, and modelling to understand cement displacement and exchange flows in irregular or inclined wellbores and help reduce leakage risks.
Influence of Substrate Roughness on Spreading of Viscoplastic Drops
Drop spreading; surface roughness; non-Newtonian fluids
Use drop-impact experiments, rheometry, high-speed imaging, image analysis, and simulations to determine how surface roughness changes the spreading and final shape of yield-stress droplets.
Undergraduate Research Opportunity in Fluid Mechanics and Rheology: squeeze cementing
Squeeze cementing; fast flow models; experiments
Develop fast, experiment-supported models of cement-slurry penetration into uncertain leakage pathways so pumping pressures and well-repair operations can be predicted and optimized.
Non-Newtonian Fluid Mechanics and How It Addresses Real-World Industrial Challenges
Primary well cementing; viscoplastic flow simulation
Process experimental images and compare reduced-order and 3D simulations to identify fluid and geometric conditions that minimize mixing between drilling mud and cement slurry.
Investigate theoretical, numerical, and graphical questions about area-critical surfaces and associated eigenvalue problems, tailored to the student's background and interests.
Distinguishing Orders in the 4-Generator Rational Exterior Algebra

Find invariants that distinguish non-isomorphic integral orders in a 16-dimensional rational exterior algebra, supporting the construction and classification of topological spaces with those cohomology rings.
Structure and Randomness Across Discrete and Continuous Worlds
Harmonic analysis; geometric measure theory; probability
Explore how combinatorial and probabilistic behaviour in finite systems connects to size, density, and structure in continuous spaces, with emphasis on examples, experimentation, and proof.
Models and Simulations of Cell Signaling and Differentiation
Extend mathematical and Morpheus-based simulations of how embryonic cells communicate, self-organize, and differentiate, including how mutations alter collective signalling and fate decisions.
Homogeneous dynamics; adelic groups; measure theory
Investigate how measure-classification results for real homogeneous spaces can be adapted to adelic quotients involving rational points and p-adic components.
Exploring and Clustering Scanned Images of Handwritten Mathematics
Andrew Rechnitzer; Colin Macdonald; Lindsey Daniels
Machine learning; handwriting recognition; education technology

Develop methods to recognize and cluster short handwritten mathematical responses, build an open dataset, and potentially integrate the results into UBC Math's Plom marking system with privacy and ethics safeguards.
Extend BlockTensorFactorization.jl with automatic differentiation so users can define custom factorization models and objective functions, then validate the framework on synthetic and real data.
Develop and implement methods that estimate a matrix's underlying rank from noisy data by modelling the noise-driven distribution of singular values.
Implement faster maximum-entropy density estimation in DualPerspective.jl using Gauss-Legendre and Gauss-Hermite quadrature for efficient gradient and Hessian evaluation.

values: UBC Mathematics Undergraduate Research Projects — 2025 Projects
Public institutional emails were verified against current UBC or UBC-hosted directory/profile pages. Project synopses are concise paraphrases of the official listing.
Study generalizations of Schur functions generated by Young-tableau-like objects, seeking new algebraic and combinatorial properties and connections to other areas.
Research in Fluid Mechanics and Rheology
Well cementing; complex and viscoplastic fluids

Use laboratory measurements and modelling to understand Newtonian and yield-stress fluid displacement in wellbores, including wall irregularity, casing motion, and inclination, to reduce methane leakage.
The Amitsur-Levitsky Theorem and the Dixon-Pressman Conjecture
Seek a Pauli-matrix proof of the Amitsur-Levitsky theorem and use computer algebra experiments to identify patterns that could advance the odd-degree Dixon-Pressman conjecture.
Mathematical Modelling of Antibiotic Resistance: Nutrients, Competition, and Evolutionary Dynamics Within a Host
Antibiotic resistance; optimal control; within-host dynamics

Create and analyze a model of sensitive and resistant bacteria, immunity, and nutrient competition, then optimize antibiotic and nutrient strategies that suppress infection while limiting treatment costs and side effects.
Geometry and Analysis of the Space of Probability Measures
Optimal transport; probability; geometry and data science
Explore theoretical and computational questions about probability distributions through optimal transport, including gradient flows, statistical inference, and constrained curve or manifold fitting.
Understanding the Role of Social Network Dynamics on Adolescent Tobacco and Nicotine Use
Public-health modelling; social networks; tobacco use

Use national survey data and mathematical simulations to identify how peer influence, network topology, marketing, and environmental factors drive youth tobacco and nicotine use and to inform targeted prevention.
Developing a 3D Geometric Bulk-Surface PDE Immune-Tumour Interactions Model
Cancer modelling; bulk-surface PDEs; finite elements
Formulate and solve a predictive 3D model of tumour spheroids interacting with immune cells, estimating quantities such as infiltration, roundness, and growth or decay against experimental studies.
Analysis of a New Moving-Boundary Two-Stage Patterning Model for Conifer Cotyledon Whorl Morphogenesis
Use linear stability analysis and nonlinear simulations to study diffusion-driven two-stage pattern formation on a deforming hemispherical surface representing a developing conifer structure.
Study neutrophil swarming, cellular mechanical responses, or a related group topic through literature review, mathematical modelling, and Python or Morpheus simulations.
Skyrmionic Structures in Models of Chiral Magnetic Materials
Investigate stability, energy landscapes, and phase transitions of skyrmions and skyrmion lattices using literature review, numerical and asymptotic calculations, and rigorous analysis.
Soliton-Potential Interactions in Nonlinear Schrödinger Equations
Study how external potentials affect strongly interacting soliton pairs through an associated linear eigenvalue problem, combining computation, asymptotics, and analysis.
Projects in Computational Geometry and/or Computational Statistics
Investigate particle-packing configuration spaces or optimize trajectories of stochastic particles using computation, data analysis, geometry, probability, and automatic-differentiation tools.
Compare tensor formats within MACE-style models for particle interactions, evaluating mathematical expressivity, computational performance, and learning dynamics in Python or Julia.
Projects on Optimization Algorithms for Data Science and Machine Learning

Develop theory for stochastic minimization and min-max algorithms and/or implement and benchmark them on applications such as robust optimization, reinforcement learning, linear programming, and GAN training.
Cracking the Code: Discovering the Mathematics Behind Patterns
Patterns; analysis; geometry; combinatorics and probability
Use a broad mathematical toolkit to investigate hidden structure in patterns, including questions about geometric configurations, the size and density of sets, and pattern-recognition applications.
P-adic Incidence Theorems and Hyperplane Functions
Additive combinatorics; p-adic geometry; harmonic analysis
Develop p-adic analogues of incidence theorems over finite p-adic rings, potentially adapting polynomial methods to a setting between Euclidean and finite-field geometry.
Analyzing Developmental Stochastic Processes with Optimal Transport
Single-cell biology; optimal transport; developmental dynamics
Model cell differentiation as a time-varying probability distribution in gene-expression space and develop algorithms that reconstruct developmental trajectories from single-cell samples.
Prove that a proposed collection of features is sufficient to decide whether two planar point clouds are equivalent under translations, rotations, and reflections.
Nonlinear data fitting; algebraic geometry; PCA generalization
Find low-degree curves, hypersurfaces, or varieties that best fit point-cloud data by minimizing squared distance, beginning with quadratic curves in the plane.
Learn identification tools such as the back-door and front-door criteria, then re-derive or extend them using algebraic methods for situations where direct interventions are impossible or unethical.
Cover Times by Multiple Random Walks

Determine expected cover times and concentration behaviour for multiple independent walks on vertex-transitive graphs, using graph statistics and links to geometric group theory and the Gaussian free field.
Estimating Markov Chain Parameters Using Multiple Walks
Markov chains; randomized algorithms; spectral methods
Design and analyze algorithms that use multiple random walks to estimate mixing times, spectral gaps, spectral measures, and non-reversible analogues, including concentration bounds.
Numerical Implementation of Radon and X-Ray Transforms for Reconstruction from Point Cloud Data
Medical imaging; inverse problems; compressed sensing

Implement forward and inverse Radon and X-ray transforms on randomly sampled point clouds, beginning with interpolation and filtered back-projection and extending to sparse-regularized reconstruction.
Image and Data Analysis for Improving Feedback from Digital Grading of Handwritten Work
Lindsey Daniels; Colin Macdonald; Andrew Rechnitzer
Education technology; image analysis; machine learning
Develop tools to extract and cluster written responses, recognize characters, and generate individualized feedback from scanned assessments while foregrounding ethics and privacy.
Learn the Lean language and formalize selected mathematical results—potentially in measure rigidity, algebraic number theory, Galois theory, or Fourier analysis—to create machine-verified proofs.
Investigate whether regularized optimal transport can exploit structure in a physical state space to improve reconstruction of an unknown state vector from observations.
